diagrams.title: PUE Formula Visualization
diagrams.description: Shows how PUE is calculated as a ratio of total facility power to IT equipment power
elements: Total Facility Power Input (includes cooling, lighting, IT equipment)
diagrams.purpose: Understanding data center efficiency metric calculation

diagrams.title: Hot/Cold Aisle Containment
diagrams.description: Illustrates proper airflow management in data centers with alternating hot and cold aisles

diagrams.title: Virtualization Architecture
diagrams.description: Shows how virtualization layer creates multiple virtual machines on single physical hardware
diagrams.purpose: Understanding virtualization concept and resource sharing

diagrams.title: Thin Client vs Traditional Desktop
diagrams.description: Side-by-side comparison of power consumption and component distribution
elements: Traditional PC: Local CPU, RAM, Storage, PSU (150-300W)
Thin Client: Minimal components, Display only (5-15W)
diagrams.purpose: Comparing energy consumption of different endpoint solutions

diagrams.title: Server Consolidation Before/After
diagrams.description: Visual comparison showing reduction in physical servers through consolidation
elements: Before: 10 servers at 20% utilization each (total 200% capacity, high power)
After: 2 servers at 100% utilization (same capacity, 80% less power)
